articles.title: JPMorgan's Next Frontier: Prediction Markets, Promise and Peril
articles.summary: JPMorgan is exploring prediction markets as their next business opportunity, weighing the potential rewards against regulatory and reputational risks. If America's largest bank enters the space, it could legitimize prediction markets for institutional investors while potentially sanitizing the wild west culture that makes them interesting.

articles.title: Betting on the verdict: Kalshi case could shape prediction markets
articles.summary: Kalshi is fighting a legal battle that will determine how far prediction markets can push into traditionally regulated territory. The outcome could either unlock massive new betting categories or slam the brakes on the industry's expansion into politics and beyond.

articles.title: Amid Trump support, prediction markets face political headwinds
articles.summary: Prediction markets are catching heat from critics who claim they skewed pro-Trump during the election cycle, potentially facing regulatory pushback or public skepticism about their accuracy. The controversy could throttle mainstream adoption and invite heavier government scrutiny just as these platforms were gaining credibility with Wall Street and retail investors.

articles.title: Iran war bets turn prediction markets into real-time macro radar: Sygnum
articles.summary: Sygnum Bank is highlighting how prediction markets tracking Iran conflict probabilities have become sophisticated real-time indicators for macro economic movements. This validates the thesis that betting markets now function as genuine financial intelligence tools, not just gambling platforms, giving traders an edge over traditional news cycles.

articles.title: Prediction markets challenge tribal casinos’ hard-won place in US gambling
articles.summary: Prediction markets are butting heads with tribal casinos as they expand into territory that Native American gaming has controlled for decades. If tribal governments start lobbying against prediction markets the same way they've fought other gambling expansion, it could create serious regulatory headwinds in key states.

articles.title: ‘Dystopian death market’: Polymarket halts bets regarding US airman after Moulton condemns site
articles.summary: Polymarket suspended betting markets related to a US airman's death after facing criticism from Representative Seth Moulton, who called the platform a "dystopian death market." The controversy highlights growing regulatory pressure on prediction markets that touch sensitive topics, potentially forcing platforms to narrow their scope to avoid political backlash.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Create New Insider Trading Headaches for Executives
articles.summary: Corporate executives now face murky legal territory when betting on prediction markets that could be influenced by their inside knowledge. The uncertainty could either drive away institutional players or create a compliance nightmare that slows mainstream adoption of prediction markets.

articles.title: Polymarket removes wagers on U.S. service member rescue mission in Iran
articles.summary: Polymarket pulled betting markets on whether U.S. forces would launch a rescue operation for American service members reportedly held in Iran. The move highlights how prediction platforms walk a tightrope between offering newsworthy markets and avoiding bets that could compromise actual military operations or national security.

articles.title: The 4 biggest differences between Kalshi and Polymarket
articles.summary: Kalshi and Polymarket have emerged as the two dominant prediction market platforms, but they operate under vastly different regulatory frameworks, user bases, betting limits, and market selection strategies. These differences will determine which platform captures more serious money and mainstream adoption as prediction markets go from niche hobby to legitimate financial instrument.

articles.title: Prediction Market Odds for Next Attorney General
articles.summary: Prediction markets are pricing the odds on who Trump will pick as his next Attorney General, with bettors weighing legal backgrounds against loyalty tests. The AG pick carries massive implications for everything from antitrust enforcement to election law, making it a high-stakes bet that could move fast once Trump signals his preference.

articles.title: Can anything stop Kalshi?
articles.summary: Kalshi continues its aggressive expansion into new prediction markets, seemingly unstoppable as it captures more trading volume and launches increasingly diverse betting categories. Their dominance could reshape how Americans think about prediction markets, potentially forcing competitors like Polymarket to pivot or risk getting steamrolled by Kalshi's regulatory advantages and mainstream appeal.

articles.title: Prediction markets: the hunt for the new ‘dumb money’
articles.summary: Prediction markets are actively seeking out inexperienced bettors who consistently make poor wagering decisions, essentially hunting for profitable counterparties to exploit. This represents a maturation of the industry from idealistic "wisdom of crowds" rhetoric toward the cold reality that someone has to lose money for others to profit consistently.
